task_completion.description: Open the 'InternationalApp' project and its files in Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE is open, showing the 'InternationalApp' project in the Package Explorer, and either 'WindowsService.java' or 'messages_jp.properties' is opened in the editor.
With 'WindowsService.java' active, the menu 'File > Convert Line Delimiters To > Unix' is selected, OR the bottom-right status bar in Eclipse shows 'LF' instead of 'CRLF'.

task_completion.description: Change the project text encoding to UTF-8.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Properties for InternationalApp' dialog is open, and in the 'Resource' section, the 'Text file encoding' is set to 'Other: UTF-8'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Japanese localization file displays correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'messages_jp.properties' file is visible in the editor and displays correct Japanese characters (e.g., ようこそ, エラー, 保存) without any garbled text (mojibake).

task_completion.description: Save all changes made to the files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no asterisks (*) next to the file names in the editor tabs, indicating all changes have been saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Eclipse IDE menus and dialogs, not terminal commands like 'dos2unix' or 'iconv'.

integrity.description: The changes were made to the correct project in Eclipse.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Package Explorer and Properties dialog clearly show the 'InternationalApp' project being modified.

integrity.description: The agent fixed the encoding rather than manually retyping the Japanese text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen changing the project encoding in the Properties dialog, rather than manually typing or pasting the Japanese characters into the editor to replace the mojibake.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is 'InternationalApp'. The Java file is 'src/com/legacy/WindowsService.java' which initially has CRLF line endings. The properties file is 'src/messages_jp.properties' which contains UTF-8 Japanese text (welcome=ようこそ, error=エラー, save=保存) but is initially loaded with ISO-8859-1 encoding, causing mojibake. The agent must use Eclipse to convert the Java file's line delimiters to Unix (LF) and change the project's text file encoding to UTF-8.
